Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Sep-02-2009 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-24-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Kathy Heckard and Henry Gordon, Jr. Victoria Hall for Rosie Harper. Gilbert Jay for Rosie Lee Harper (Administrator). Tracy Potts appeared by telephone for Brandeburger & Davis ("B & D"). Willie Howard (Niece) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Conrad informed the Court that he would be filing a motion to withdraw as Counsel due to a conflict and that all parties had been notified. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Brandeburger & Davis' claims. The Court directed Mr. Jay to file a separate noticed petition for the evidentiary hearing regarding the assignments to B & D pursuant to probate code 11604. The Court granted the petition, (subject to review of the order). The Court approved a preliminary distribution to the Parties not affected by the evidentiary hearing. The Court approved 75% of the statutory fees. The remaining 25% is to be held pending the outcome of the evidentiary hearing.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-02-2009 Continued To Jun-23-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Oct-08-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: Gilbert Jay for Rosie Harper (Current Administrator).

Mr. Jay stated that he received a call from Laurie Arnold stating she would be unable to appear at today's hearing, and informed the Court that she had approved of the proposed order. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Sep-24-2008 Continued To Oct-08-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar Aug-25-2008 Off Calendar

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (474-476 Waller St.S.F.)
Off Calendar Jul-07-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gilbert Jay for Rosie Harper. Lori Arnold for Alvin Gordon (Trustee).

The sale was confirmed at $504,000.00 for 50% interest in the property. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court). The matter was set for August 25, 2008 for status on filing of the receipt for the blocked account.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Jun-18-2008 Continued To Sep-24-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gilbert Jay for and with Rosie Harper. Lori Arnold for Alvin Gordon (Former Administrator).

Mr. Jay stated that there was a miscommunication with regard to the order after hearing of May 21st and the continued date of June 4th and presented the Order Concerning The First Account Of Former Administrator which the Court signed. The Parties agreed to go to mediation. Mr. Jay informed the Court that an offer on the property had been obtained and was to be heard on July 7, 2008. The matter was continued to September 24, 2008 for status on mediation.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith and A. Santiago.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Jun-04-2008 Continued To Jun-18-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person.

The matter was continued to June 18, 2008. The Clerk will notify all Counsel to appear on that date.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Maria Lertora CSR#11935. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Aquino.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar May-21-2008 Continued To Jun-04-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: George McNitt for Yvette Gordon (Heir). Victoria Hall and Gilbert Jay for and with Rosie Harper.

The Former Administrator and Counsel were not present. Ms. Hall stated that there was a concern about missing rents. Mr. Jay suggested taking the matter off calendar, that the money be turned over to his client and having the bond for Mr. Gordon reduced to $100,000.00. The Court continued the matter to June 4, 2008. Mr. Jay is to contact Mr. Goins to advise him of the hearing date and to have his client appear on that date as well. The bond for Mr. Gordon is reduced to $100,000.00. The money is to be turned over to Ms. Harper. Mr. Jay will submit the order after hearing. Ms. Hall reported that the property had been appraised and that an offer had been received.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: B. Mann.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Apr-30-2008 Continued To May-21-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Feb-26-2008 Continued To Apr-30-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lori Arnold for Alvin Gordon (Petitioner). Victoria Hall appeared by telephone for Rosie Harper (Objector), present in court.

Ms. Hall reported that her client was going to sign a 90 day listing agreement with the agent. In addition, she stated that Mr. Gordon should have collected receipts from the Grandmother. Furthermore, Ms. Hall suggested Ms. Arnold request information from the Housing Authority, to which Ms. Arnold agreed. The Court will sign a search and order. Ms. Arnold stated that Mr. Gordon admitted to co-mingling the funds, and that the three bank accounts were co-mingled as well, but that she had the original bank statements, and that she would try to part out the funds. In addition, Ms. Arnold stated that in regards to the loan, the estate was not liable to the trust as Mr. Gordon used the funds for personal reasons. Ms. Hall expressed concern about the amended account not being resolved before the sale of the house. Ms. Arnold stated that the creditor's claim would be taken care of when the property was sold to correct questions raised by the Examiner. Mr. Kneis informed the parties that the referee would appraise the property when it was ready for confirmation. In addition, Mr. Kneis suggested making a list of receipts and expenses to determine what belongs to the estate. The matter was continued to April 30, 2008 for filing of the amended account.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Jan-22-2008 Continued To Feb-26-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lori Arnold for Alvin Gordon (Former Administrator). Victoria Hall (appeared by phone) for Rosie Harper (present in court).

Ms. Hall stated objections to the amendments and explanation of the first accounts filed by Mr. Gordon. The matter was continued to February 26, 2008 so that the examiners can review the amendment and explanations. Objections should be communicated to counsel and all notices are to be given. Responses should be received before the court date of February 26, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Dec-05-2007 Continued To Jan-22-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Yasmin Gilani for Alvin Gordon (Petitioner). Victoria Hall by phone for Rosie Harper (present) and Willie Gordon.

Ms. Harper has not obtained the bond. Ms. Hall stated that if they were not able to get a bond, she would file an ex-parte petition to modify the order for limited powers. Ms. Gilani requested time to give notice, and to address and the accounting. Counsel is to send notice by December 21, 2007. The matter was continued to January 22, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Julia Capozzoli.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Alvin Gordon'S Final Account
Off Calendar Oct-24-2007 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Revoking Authority Of Personal Representative To Administer The Estate, For Suspension Of Powers, And For Removal Of Representative
Off Calendar Aug-29-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Daniel Conrad appearing for Henry Gordon, Jr. and Kathy Heckard. George McNitt appearing for Yvette Gordon. Alana Grace Connor appearing for and with administrator Alvin Gordon. Victoria Hall appearing for petitioners Willie Gordon, Sarah Robinson & Dorothy Fobbs. Their attorney in fact Rosie Harper is in court. Ms. Connor filed her opposition this morning. The administrator is ready to put the duplex on the market for sale to pay off debts. Ms. Hall argued that one of the heir has died and she did not get to enjoy her share. Issues addressed. Mr. McNitt and Mr. Conrad are in support of the petition for removal. Matter submitted.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto CSR# 5229. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: A. Aguerre.

At 10:21am, All parties returned to court except Mr. Conrad. He is in agreement with the stipulation. Alvin Gordon will resign as the personal representative. Rosie Harper will be appointed as the successor administrator. Bond is fixed in the amount of $500,000.00 until there are waivers of bond. Mr. Gordon to file his final account by 10/24. Ms. Hall will prepare the order.
